MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents From Defendant Board Of Trustees Of California State University, To Compel Answers From Witness Michael Martin, And For Monetary Sanctions Under C.C.P. 2025.480 And 2031.310
Off Calendar May-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Schwartz of Bryan Schwartz Law telephone number 510-444-9300 for Plaintiff Aaron Nevatt.
Brendan Brownfield of Burnham Brown telephone number 510-444-6800 for Defendant The Board of Trustees of California State University.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AARON NEVATT'S MOTION To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents From Defendant Board Of Trustees Of California State University, To Compel Answers From Witness Michael Martin, And For Monetary Sanctions is granted in part. Defendant The Board of Trustees must produce all documents in its possession, custody and control regarding statements made by or in the presence of Mr. Nevatt regarding the actual or possible closure of the science building that were prepared prior to the termination of Mr. Nevatt's employment that are responsive to the document requests at issue on this motion. The Board waived any privilege it had as to those documents by failing to object to Mr. Nevatt's public disclosure of the contents of the January 10, 2014 meeting when it had the means and opportunity to do so and by asking Mr. Nevatt questions at his deposition about these matters. The Board must also supplement its privilege log by identifying the roles of the persons whom documents on the log were distributed and a description of the contents of those documents sufficient for the court to evaluate the Board's assertion that those documents are privileged. The Board must also make Mr. Martin available for a further deposition to answer all questions previously asked of him for which he was instructed not to answer regarding the science building and reasonable follow-up question. In all other respects Mr. Nevatt's motion is denied without prejudice, including his request for sanctions. The Board had substantial justification for all positions it took on this motion. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Jaime Hopp, CSR #13751, 925-759-0770. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Schwartz of Bryan Schwartz Law telephone number 510-444-9300 for Plaintiff Aaron Nevatt.
Brendan Brownfield of Burnham Brown telephone number 510-444-6800 for Defendant The Board of Trustees of California State University.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BOARD OF TRUSTEES OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE UNIVERSITY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is denied in its entirety. The Board has not satisfied its burden of production showing that plaintiff Aaron Nevatt cannot prove protected activity as to any of his six claims and, even if it did, Mr. Nevatt presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he engaged in protected activity as to all of this claims other than his second cause or action. Paragraphs 15 and 20 of the first amended complaint which are incorporated into all of Mr. Nevatt's claims allege that in November 2013 Mr. Nevatt disclosed to Mr. Martin his belief that the lack of an operations and maintenance program violated state and federal laws and on January 10, 2014 Mr. Nevatt disclosed to President Wong his belief that the science building should be closed due to violations of safety regulations. These allegations are sufficient to support the requirements of protected activity for the first, third, fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action based on the disclosure of reasonably based suspicions of illegal activity and are not negated by the facts stated in the Board's separate statement. (See the Board's Facts 8-9 (the only facts about the November 2013 disclosure regarding the operations and maintenance programs) and Facts 22-26 (the only facts about the January 10, 2014 disclosure about the science building)). Paragraph 56 of the first amended complaint alleges that the information Mr. Nevatt provided to Cal/OSHA led that agency to institute proceedings against SFSU. These allegations are sufficient to support the requirement of protected activity for the second cause of action and are not negated by the facts stated in the Board's separate statement. (See the Board's Facts 27-28 (the only facts about Mr. Nevatt's disclosure to Cal/OSHA)). By his Additional Facts 1-2, 4-12, 14-15, 27-29, and 33, Mr. Nevatt provided ample evidence that he engaged in protected activity sufficient to create a triable issue regarding the protected activity requirement for his first, third, fourth, fifth and sixth causes of action based on the disclosure of reasonably based suspicions of illegal activity. The Board's objections to the evidence submitted by Mr. Nevatt cited in support of his Additional Facts 1-2, 4-12, 14-15, 27-29, and 33 are overruled. Mr. Nevatt's requests to amend his complaint and for a continuance of the hearing per CCP 437c(h) are denied as unnecessary to resolution of this motion. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
